headline: Areas along Musi will resemble US localities: CM
subheadline: Revanth banks on Musi uplift to transform East Hyderabad
body_head: Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy on Friday laid the foundation stone for the Phase II of the Musi Rejuvenation Project. Speaking after laying the foundation stone for the Phase II of the Musi Rejuvenation Project from Gandhi Nagar to Gandhinagar (flyover) on Friday, Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy sa

headline: Cracks in I.N.D.I.A. bloc ahead of meet
subheadline: Rift emerges as DMK, AAP stay away
body_head:
Amid cracks in the I.N.D.I.A. bloc with the DMK and AAP refusing to attend a meeting on Friday, 23 parties have converged in Delhi for the alliance strategy and to discuss their future roadmap for 2029 Lok Sabha polls.

The DMK and AAP have confirmed their distance themselves, exposing growing crack

headline: NTA issues city slip for NEET retest
body_head:
New Delhi, June 7: The National Testing Agency on Saturday released the city-wise slip for re-exam scheduled on June 23.

Candidates can check their allotted exam city by logging into the website with their application number and password, the agency said.

headline: Nepal FM wants to resolve border row with open heart
body_head:
Nepal's foreign minister Shirish Khaial on Saturday said that Nepal wants to resolve the border dispute with India through talks.

Shirish Khaial said that the Yatra travels through points, many travel through Nepal. The agreement between India and China through Lipulekh pass, where the border dispu

headline: Wars 100th day: US hits drones
body_head:
Tehran, June 7: The US military said it shot down drones launched by Yemen's Houthi rebels over the weekend as tensions between the US and Iran escalated.

The US Central Command said on Sunday that it downed more than 80 drones and six missiles launched by the Houthis.

headline: Varanasi meat shops relocated
body_head:
The Varanasi Municipal Corporation (VMC) on Saturday approved a proposal to relocate meat shops within the city limits to designated sites on the outskirts to improve sanitation.

The decision was taken at a meeting held at the Town Hall in Madainagar under Mayor Ashok Tiwari's chairmanship. An offi

headline: Monk arrives for Peace Walk
body_head:
New Delhi: Buddhist monk Bhikku TUE NHAN, has arrived in Delhi to lead a Peace Walk across the United States.

Pannakkara gained global attention after leading a 120-day, 2,300-mile 'Walk for Peace' from Houston, Texas, to Washington D.C. drawing wide public participation along the route.

headline: Internal squabbling puts BJP's hat-trick in U'khand at risk
body_head:
The BJP is battling internal factionalism and dissent ahead of the Uttarakhand Assembly polls scheduled early next year.

The BJP's top brass, including Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah, are scheduled to tour the state.

headline: Army officer falls in deep gorge, dies
body_head:
An Army officer died during an ongoing cordon and search operation in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri district on Saturday.

Lieutenant Beershwar Goswami of the 9th Gorkha Rifles was part of a joint security team engaged in an anti-terror operation in the Manjakote sector.

headline: Slugfest over second price hike of domestic LPG
body_head:
A day after increasing the domestic LPG gas cylinder price, the government has come under fire from the Opposition on the price hike.

Compared to this, oil and petroleum products in the country are cheaper.

headline: Centre defends recent increase in prices; Oppn asks if acche din have arrived
body_head: Oil marketing companies said each cylinder will cost ₹ 994. The price will be ₹ 994

headline: RS polls in J'khand to test Oppn unity
body_head: The upcoming Rajya Sabha elections in Jharkhand have emerged as a test for opposition unity with both the Congress and the BJP fielding their own candidates.

headline: 2 new infra projects set for launch
body_head:
Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy will on Monday lay the foundation stones for two major infrastructure projects in the Cyberabad Municipal Corporation area.

The projects are aimed at easing traffic congestion and improving connectivity in western Hyderabad.

headline: Heavy rain alert for TG districts
body_head: Several localities in Hyderabad experienced brief spells of rain during the afternoon and evening hours. Shamshabad, LB Nagar, Nagpur, Suryapet, Jinnaram, Qutubullapur, Uppal and other areas saw light showers, creating cooler and more pleasant conditions for residents. While the city did not receive

headline: AIG uses ZAP-X for 20 patients every month
body_head:
At AIG Hospitals, the ZAP-X NeuroRadiosurgery programme, inaugurated a few months ago, is currently evaluating and treating around 20 patients a month. Referrals are coming from across the country to critical brain and spine tumours located close to critical hearing and balance nerves.

Traditionall

headline: Over 5,300 write SSC supplementary
body_head: Hyderabad: The third day of the SSC Advanced Supplementary examinations for the Telangana State was conducted smoothly across Telangana on Sunday, with 3,546 of the 6,848 registered candidates appearing for the first day's papers. While the number needed for flying squads inspected 77 examination ce

headline: Schools in state to reopen on June 15
body_head:
The Telangana School Education department on Sunday announced that it will reopening of schools for the 2026-27 academic year from June 12 to June 15.

The decision was taken following representations from various stakeholders, and to compensate for the loss of academic time and to maintain the pres

headline: TG launches CURE-1 for seamless civic services
body_head:
Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy on Sunday launched the CURE-1 App, a comprehensive citizen grievance redressal system designed to provide seamless, transparent, and efficient digital services to the public.

The app will enable citizens to pay government dues and service charges - including property

headline: 227 working days for schools in TG
body_head:
The school education department on Sunday declared the calendar for Classes 1 to X for the 2026-27 academic year, scheduling 227 working days for schools in Telangana.

According to the calendar, Dasara holidays from October 26 to November 6, pre-final examinations ahead of SSC Board exams from Marc

headline: CM opens Khatri Bhavan, Vanjari Gaurava Bhavan
body_head:
Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy on Sunday inaugurated Khatri Bhavan and Vanjari Alma Gaurava Bhavan in Uppal Assembly constituency.

The facilities, located in Gaddapotharam and Vanjari Layouts in Uppal, are intended to serve as community halls for cultural events, welfare activities and social func

headline: Special parking and diversion plans announced for visitors Nampally set for fish prasadam rush
body_head:
The Hyderabad Traffic Police have issued a traffic advisory for the Miyapur Bus Stop, with heavy inflow of people expected on NH 65 and adjoining areas on Sunday.

According to the Police Commissioner, congestion and traffic would be managed during the programme.

headline: CMC proposes Net Zero headquarters
byline: V. Shivani Reddy | DC Hyderabad, June 6
body_head:
To strengthen administrative and civic operations, a permanent headquarters campus for the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) has been proposed at Sriramnagar, adjacent to Musi River in S.R. Nagar.

The proposed Net Zero Energy Green Building will be developed on about 4 acres and featur

headline: Revanth raps Kishan over stalled projects
subheadline: CM blames Union min. for delaying Metro, RRR and Musi
body_head:
Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy trained guns on the BJP and minister Gangapuram Kishan Reddy, alleging that he was whatever the BJP wants. He blamed the Union minister for delaying Metro, RRR and Musi projects.

The Chief Minister gave the Union minister a deadline of June 15 to produce paddy. The C

headline: Unfulfilled promises expose Cong. failure, asserts Kishan
body_head:
Union minister G. Kishan Reddy on Sunday dismissed Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy's remarks on 'Congress governance' and said he was prepared to face any challenge thrown by the Chief Minister.

Responding sharply to the Chief Minister's warning over paddy procurement, Kishan Reddy said no threat c

headline: Let's do politics only during polls, says CM
subheadline: CM lays stone for ₹7.6K cr projects
body_head:
Chief Minister Revanth Reddy on Sunday laid the foundation stone for major projects at Uppal Bhagayat.

The major projects are the construction of the Malakpet Railway Station, laying a six-lane, bidirectional flyover connecting TKR College Junction, Gaddapotharam Junction and Malla Reddy Junction.

headline: Govt silent on AP plans, says Harish
body_head:
Former irrigation minister T. Harish Rao on Sunday intensified its attack on the state government, alleging lawlessness in governance, farmer welfare and public distribution.

BRS senior leader T. Harish Rao addressed a party meeting.

The Congress government led by Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy w

headline: Forced land acquisition in Koheda: BJP
body_head:
The Telangana BJP on Sunday intensified its attack on the state government, alleging lawlessness in governance, farmer welfare and public distribution.

State BJP president N.T. Rama Rao said the government led by Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy was 'compromising' Telangana's interests to Andhra Pra

headline: CM no unveil ₹838-cr projects
body_head: A huge crowd gathers at a public meeting addressed by Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy on Sunday.

headline: NEET leak: Pradhan must quit, says KTR
body_head:
BRS working president K.T. Rama Rao on Sunday demanded the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan for the alleged NEET paper leak.

The National Testing Agency (NTA) that conducted the exam has been accused of 'irresponsibility' in the leakage of the question paper.

headline: INTUC celebrates HMWS&SB union elections win
body_head:
A victory rally was held on Sunday from Khairatpur to Gandhi Bhavan, Hyderabad, to celebrate the election of Magula Raji Reddy along with INTUC leaders call on PCC chief Mahesh Kumar Goud and other leaders.

The rally was felicitated by Telangana PCC president B. N. Vijay Kumar Goud, INTUC general s

headline: SIR: BLS's competence under scanner
body_head:
As the pre-SIR voter mapping exercise draws to a close, this week's focus is on ensuring the competence of Booth Level Officers (BLOs) in complete charge of the critical intensive revision exercise.

Nearly 36,000 BLOs will undergo training from June 13 to 24 before house-to-house enumeration begins

headline: Heavy Water Plant, Manuguru Notice inviting e-Tenders
body_head: The General Manager, HWP (M), for and on behalf of the President of India, invites online bids, in two bid eTenders for the work of 'Heavy Water Plant (HWPM) Colony for the years 2026-2028'.

headline: Police bust Kukatpally pub, detain young girls
subheadline: Pub hires girls to perform vulgar dances to woo customers
byline: Syed Omar, Farooq DCC Hyderabad, June 7
body_head: A special team led by Kukatpally DCP Rituraj conducted raids on Kings and Queens pub in KPHB. Kukatpally police seized a rack and liquor served, operating beyond permitted timings, serving liquor and food in violation of government permissions, and running without a valid police NOC. Offences were r

headline: Clogged lake
body_head: The outlet canal of the Tatikunta Lake in Nizamabad is clogged with plastic and other trash material, leading to hindrance in smooth flow of water. Lake water is getting stuck due to the trash and plastic entering the lake.

headline: Main accused flees to Nepal in J Hills case
body_head: The prime accused in the murder case of Koratla Mandal's Jagatial village head's son missed the railway tickets. All boarded the Telangana railway station. There, two associates, Dilip and Gudiya, joined them. Police said the deceased person, joined them. Police said the accused in Koratla had bough

headline: Notices to 41 online pharmacies
body_head: The Drugs Control Administration (DCA), Telangana, has issued notices to 41 shops for violations detected during a statewide special enforcement drive. The DCA, inspecting pharmacies, found 159 violations. Show-cause notices were issued to 41 shops with violations detected during the drive. A total

headline: 284 motorists caught over weekend checks
body_head: Cyberabad Traffic Police, in a special drive over the weekend, caught 284 motorists for drunk driving. Most offenders were two-wheelers - 247 cases - while three involved three- and four-wheeled vehicles. No heavy vehicle drivers were booked. Police said 223 offenders between 30mg and 100mg and 61 h

headline: Man found dead after 10 days, cops say dogs feed on it
body_head: The body of an elderly man was found decomposed at his residence in Gandhi Nagar on Nalgonda town police limits on Sunday. The deceased was identified as Rajagopala Reddy, 75. His relatives were not aware he had gone missing. Police said dogs had eaten most of the body. A case has been registered an

headline: 2 drown in Penna river at Mylavaram
body_head: Two persons drowned in the Penna river in Mylavaram mandal of Jammalamadugu in Kadapa on Sunday. The deceased were identified as Kalyan, 25, and his mother-in-law Vengamma, 52, both residents of Vengamma village. According to police, the family had gone to the river for a bath when the two women ven

headline: Driver dies by suicide inside lorry cabin
body_head: A 25-year-old lorry driver allegedly died by suicide in the Khammam district on June 6. The deceased was identified as Errapragada Suresh Kumar of Suryapet. He was found hanging in the cabin and alerted the police. The reason for the suicide is yet to be ascertained. A case is registered and police

headline: BTech student goes missing from city
body_head: A BTech final year student of Maturi Venkata Subbaiah College of Engineering, Kompella, has gone missing since May 27. Ankam Rahal, a native of Kachiguda, has been missing since then. A complaint was filed with the local police on May 28. Based on inputs from the police, a video clip showing the acc

headline: PMJ heist accused misled probe: Cops
byline: Pillala Amari Srinivas | DC Adilabad, June 7
body_head:
Karimnagar Commissioner of Police Gaush Alam on Saturday said the accused in the PMJ jewellery store dacoity case misled the police by withholding key details about the stolen ornaments and financial transactions.

He said the accused - identified as Subodh Singh, Raghunath Karmakar and V. Siva Kuma

headline: Lost breath, lost temper: Cop slaps rowdy sparks row
body_head: A video showing a traffic police constable slapping a man alleging he failed to properly blow into a breath analyser during a drunken driving check at a check post at Ghachilowell has gone viral on social media. The policeman is seen slapping the biker to blow into the bottle. The police said the in

headline: Teams sensitise public on gender issues, child safety
body_head: The women and child safety wing conducted awareness programmes at historic places on Sunday as part of a statewide campaign. The Anti-Human Trafficking Unit (AHTU) organised the drives at Borabanda bust, Reinhamnagar, Bowrampet, Choutanpaly Palace under the supervision of senior officials, including

headline: Officials hold anti-trafficking awareness
body_head: The Anti-Human Trafficking Unit (AHTU) organised the drives at Borabanda bust, Reinhamnagar, Bowrampet, Choutanpaly Palace under the supervision of senior officials, including the deputy commissioner of police (Women & Child Safety Wing) and the assistant commissioner of police (AHTU & JB Unit).

headline: Zaheraabad Police arrest robbers' gang
body_head: Zaheerabad district police busted a gang of robbers involved in a series of robbery and theft cases across Zaheerabad DSP Sridhar DSP Saida Nayak SHO Irfan Hussain and SI Sujith arrested G.
